summary: The narrowest BM-017 slice that fit the current repo was to add one new Clasp workflow-correctness benchmark and bind it to the existing persistence benchmark through a shared correctness regression script and alias.
ergonomics: Benchmark task scaffolding is straightforward once you copy an existing task shape; `task.json` + local `scripts/verify.sh` + `test/*.mjs` is a clean contract.
`benchmarks/test-task-prep.sh` is valuable but slow and noisy because every prepare emits repeated dirty-tree warnings and some Clasp tasks trigger bootstrap compilation during prep checks.
The benchmark runner already had enough hooks for this task; adding a new alias and local verify path required no runner refactor.
follow_ups: Add a mirrored TypeScript workflow-constraint task if BM-017 should become a head-to-head comparison instead of a mixed correctness suite.
Consider teaching benchmark prep artifacts to expose workflow declarations in the context graph; the new prep assertion had to rely on AIR instead of context output.
Reduce repeated dirty-tree warning noise in benchmark scripts so failures surface faster during long `verify-all` runs.
warnings: Do not assert workflow nodes in `benchmark-prep/Main.context.json` for this task; the current prep output only exposed `Counter` schema data, not `CounterFlow` workflow IDs.
The prepared `clasp-workflow-correctness` workspace must stay intentionally incomplete; copying the solution `Main.clasp` is what should flip verification to green.
`scripts/verify-all.sh` now includes the new correctness benchmark smoke test, so any future change to the task scaffold or solution path will break full verification quickly.
tests_run: node benchmarks/run-benchmark.mjs prepare clasp-workflow-correctness --workspace benchmarks/workspaces/manual-correctness --allow-bootstrap-recovery true
node benchmarks/run-benchmark.mjs verify clasp-workflow-correctness --workspace benchmarks/workspaces/manual-correctness --harness local --model local --allow-bootstrap-recovery true

cp benchmarks/tasks/clasp-workflow-correctness/solution/Main.clasp benchmarks/workspaces/manual-correctness/Main.clasp && node benchmarks/run-benchmark.mjs verify clasp-workflow-correctness --workspace benchmarks/workspaces/manual-correctness --harness local --model local --allow-bootstrap-recovery true
residual_risks: The new correctness suite alias is intentionally asymmetric: it combines a Clasp workflow-correctness task with the existing TypeScript persistence task rather than a mirrored language pair for workflow constraints.
The Clasp workflow benchmark still relies on bootstrap-recovery prep and inherits the existing `MODULE_TYPELESS_PACKAGE_JSON` warning when Node loads compiled workspace artifacts.
